i'm following. based on your spending and the details of your cards, your most effective strategy is to use the capital one venture x as your primary card for almost everything. since you spend over $4,400 on home and utilities and have a large amount of uncategorized spending, the flat 2x miles on the venture x will earn you much more than the 1% you would get on your other cards for those items. your bank of america customized cash card is best used for a specific 3% category, and i recommend setting it to online shopping. this will cover your shopping and entertainment expenses and likely capture some of your digital startup costs or uncategorized online bills. however, this 3% rate is capped at $2,500 in spending per quarter. once you spend that amount, you should move all transactions back to the venture x to avoid dropping to 1% rewards. the $956 you have in bank of america rewards is substantial, and if you have at least $20,000 in a bank of america or merrill account, you may be eligible for preferred rewards which would boost that 3% rate even higher. your amex delta blue is your weakest card for daily spending because it earns delta miles instead of flexible points, so you should only use it when you have manually activated a specific offer for a merchant like starbucks or uber. the error you saw on the amex site is simply because your card earns skymiles rather than membership rewards points.
finally, since you have the venture x, you should pay your monthly cell phone bill with it to take advantage of the $800 in protection coverage mentioned in your benefits guide.

why you see that error

how to use it anyway
even though you can't get membership rewards, you still have access to
